title: T-040 — R13 org-scoped identity columns migration on lot/work_order/quality_event/shipment/bom_item placeholder tables
prompt:
# T-040 — R13 org-scoped identity columns migration on lot/work_order/quality_event/shipment/bom_item placeholder tables

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §10, §W0-v4.3
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
ACP import shape: top-level TaskCreate only (`title`, `prompt`, `labels`, `priority`, `max_attempts`, `pipeline_name`, `pipeline_inputs`). Do not add generated ACP fields such as task_id/status/project_id. `pipeline_inputs.root_path` is mandatory.

## Goal
Create downstream business-table skeletons with the locked R13 identity columns and `org_id` RLS scope so later modules do not retrofit identity/audit fields.

## Locked Wave 0 decisions to honor
- Business data-plane scope is `org_id`; do not create new business-table `tenant_id` columns. Tenant/control-plane concepts may exist above org scope, but app/business rows, RLS policies, audit/outbox rows, role grants, and R13 skeletons use `org_id`.
- Canonical finished-good domain/event prefix is `fg.*`. `fa.*` / `FA` may appear only as an explicit legacy compatibility alias during migration, never as the primary new contract.
- Use the safe non-spoofable org-context RLS pattern. Do not rely on unsafe direct custom GUC `SET` by app users. Do not declare `LEAKPROOF` unless the implementation proves it is valid and necessary.
- Lower numeric priority is picked sooner; dependencies must be local `T-XXX` IDs. Cross-module blockers, if any, belong in `pipeline_inputs.cross_module_dependencies` and in this prompt.
- Exactly one atomic task type: `T1-schema`.

## Implementation contract
Add `packages/db/src/schema/r13-business-tables.ts` with five empty business tables: `lot`, `workOrder`, `qualityEvent`, `shipment`, `bomItem`. Each table contains only: `id` UUID PK (UUIDv7 preferred), `external_id` TEXT, `org_id` UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id), `created_at` TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), `created_by_user` UUID, `created_by_device` TEXT, `app_version` TEXT, `model_prediction_id` UUID NULL, `epcis_event_id` UUID NULL, and `schema_version` INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1. Generate migration `0009_r13_placeholder_tables.sql`; add indexes `(org_id, created_at DESC)`. Enable RLS with the same safe org-context function from T-007. No domain columns.

## Files in scope
- packages/db/src/schema/r13-business-tables.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/migrations/0009_r13_placeholder_tables.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/__tests__/r13-business-tables.test.ts [create]

## Dependencies
- T-006
- T-007

## Cross-module dependencies
- None

## Acceptance criteria
1. Migration creates lot, work_order, quality_event, shipment, bom_item with all R13 columns and `(org_id, created_at DESC)` indexes.
2. INSERT with org_id=NULL is rejected for every placeholder table.
3. App-role scoped to org A cannot read rows from org B via the T-007 policy pattern.
4. Schema/typecheck proves exported inferred types include nullable model_prediction_id and epcis_event_id.

## Test strategy
- RED: write r13-business-tables.test.ts covering column list, NOT NULL, index, RLS, and type exports before schema exists.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration` and typecheck.

## Risk red lines
- Do not add business/domain columns here.
- Do not use `tenant_id` on these business tables.
- Do not skip RLS or app-role tests.

## Closeout evidence
Provide changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status, deviations_from_prd, and git_status.
